MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff, Interrogatory Responses, And Document Production
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. ARVIN ARTONIAN, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of SEDAGHAT LAW GROUP, APC (tel: 424-77-0078) for Plaintiff.
Atty. WILLIAM LAYNE, Esq. of The City of San Francisco Attorney's Office (tel: 415-554-4217) for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff, Interrogatory Responses, And Document Production.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications.

The City and County of San Francisco's unopposed motion to compel further discovery responses from plaintiff Marie Bryant is granted in part and denied in part.

The City moves to compel responses to its form interrogatories, to which no response is provided. The motion is granted. (CCP 2030.290(b).) Bryant shall provide verified responses within five court days of entry of this order.

The City moves to compel further responses to its special interrogatories and requests for production of documents. Bryant's responses were unverified and, the City contends, inadequate. The City did not supply a separate statement with its moving papers and the court denies the request to compel further responses on that basis. (Rules of Court 3.1345(a).) However, the court orders Bryant to provide verifications of her previous responses within five court days of entry of this order.

The City moves to compel Bryant's deposition, which was noticed and for which she did not appear. CCP 2025.450(a) provides that, "[i]f, after service of a deposition notice, a party to the action . . ., without having served a valid objection under Section 2025.410, fails to appear for examination . . . the party giving the notice may move for an order compelling the deponent's attendance and testimony." Subdivision (b)(2) provides that any motion to compel a deposition pursuant to 2025.450 "shall be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration under Section 2016.040, or, when the deponent fails to attend the deposition and produce the documents, electronically stored information, or things described in the deposition notice, by a declaration stating that the petitioner has contacted the deponent to inquire about the nonappearance." The City's moving papers do not include a declaration stating that the petitioner has contacted the deponent to inquire about the nonappearance. The court therefore denies the City's motion to compel Bryant to submit to deposition pursuant to its notice.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
